course_title: Genres, Forms, and Themes in Creative Writing
description:
Offered: annually
This writing-intensive workshop course combines theory and practice for an in-depth exploration of a specific genre, form, or theme in creative writing (previous course topics have included environmental writing, geography of story, and black feminist discourse). Students will learn and apply new writing skills and techniques to craft original creative pieces informed by the course topic. Their writing will represent deep investigation and advanced understanding of the chosen genre, form, or theme.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200 or HON 1200
Note: This course can be taken twice given different course topics.
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA
